name: REGEXP_LIKE
shortDescription: Test whether a string matches a regular expression
description: Returns TRUE when the source string matches the given regular expression pattern. An optional flags string controls matching behavior.

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description: Optional match flags: 'i' (case-insensitive), 'n' (dotall, '.' matches newlines), 'c' (case-sensitive, the default)

examples.title: Match a pattern
examples.description: Rows whose code starts with two letters followed by digits
examples.sql: SELECT code FROM products WHERE REGEXP_LIKE(code, '^[A-Z]{2}[0-9]+')

examples.title: Case-insensitive match
examples.description: Match regardless of letter case using the 'i' flag
examples.sql: SELECT name FROM contacts WHERE REGEXP_LIKE(name, 'smith', 'i')

notes: REGEXP is an accepted alias for REGEXP_LIKE
